yesterdayAnalysis: Yesterday's 6.4-mile run at HR 141 was not the scheduled workout (Thu called for bike 45min with 3x8min race-pace intervals). This appears to be Thursday's bike session replaced with a run, which means the last race-pace bike stimulus before Oregon taper was skipped. The run effort itself was moderate and appropriate for this phase, but missing the final race-effort bike work is a concern with only 16 days to Oregon.
todayRecommendation: Execute today's easy-moderate bike ride as planned — this is a pure shakeout session with no intensity. Keep it 45-60 minutes, zones 1-2, to preserve legs for tomorrow's 10K race while maintaining bike feel heading into taper.
weekProgress: Week 11 of 13 is at 187 of 340 TSS target (55%) with 2 days remaining. With Saturday's 10K race and Sunday's OWS still ahead, this percentage is normal for Friday in a back-loaded week. However, Thursday's scheduled race-pace bike intervals (3x8min @ 145-152w) were replaced with a run, meaning the final bike-specific race stimulus was missed before taper begins.
keyInsight: Missing Thursday's race-pace bike intervals removes the last opportunity to dial in NP 145-152w effort before Oregon in 16 days — focus taper on conservative pacing rehearsal mentally.
